blockingIssues.description: Intent deduplication is not company-scoped, so one tenant can supersede queued intents from another tenant when dedupe keys collide.
Intent state transitions use read-then-write updates without previous-state predicates, allowing concurrent TOCTOU races to violate the state machine.
Issue assignment wakeups still use legacy heartbeat wakeup paths in production call sites instead of creating issue_assigned intents.
Assignment and timer dedupe keys are incompatible, preventing assignment intents from superseding timer hints for the same issue.
Scheduler immediately consumes intents after admission/run creation, skipping the required admitted state lifecycle.
Scheduler admission lookups are not consistently company-scoped, creating cross-company data access and isolation risk.
Intents with future notBefore are rejected instead of skipped/deferred until they become due.
Lease renewal does not preserve original custom TTL after the first renewal and can inflate renewed leases to default TTL.
One-active-lease enforcement uses non-atomic check-then-insert logic, so concurrent grant attempts can create multiple active leases.
Production scheduler path still builds envelopes/runs directly and does not invoke dispatcherService.dispatchRun as the authoritative dispatch flow.
Heartbeat executeRun still resolves workspace/context inline instead of using only pre-constructed envelope context.
Runs without envelopes are still executable because runtime does not enforce requireEnvelopeForRun in the active execution path.
New dispatcher issue/workspace lookups omit companyId filters, violating multi-tenant company-scope invariants.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require validator commands to be run from `.factory/services.yaml` verbatim (including configured max-workers), and require handoff to call out any justified deviations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple hard-invariants reviews observed workers using `pnpm test:run --max-workers=4` while services.yaml canonical test command is `pnpm test:run --max-workers=1`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify and enforce the red-phase expectation (tests first, expected fail before implementation), or explicitly relax it so skill feedback is consistent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews flagged service implementation before first test execution while handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit implementation checklist item for company-scoped query predicates on all service lookups and updates.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Blocking company-scope violations were found in scheduler and dispatcher feature implementations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a canonical dispatch-intent dedupeKey convention for cross-intent supersession semantics (e.g., issue-scoped keys shared across timer/assignment/event intents where supersession is required).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Timer and assignment intent features used incompatible dedupe keys (`timer:<issueId>` vs `assignment:<issueId>`), causing required supersession behavior gaps.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated reports that services.yaml and worker test commands diverged.
Repeated reports about backend-worker red-phase/test-first adherence mismatch.
